skill.name: youtube-video-api-skill
skill.description: This skill helps users automatically extract channel-level and video detail data from a specific YouTube channel via BrowserAct API. Agent should proactively apply this skill when users express needs like extracting channel video data, getting latest or popular videos from a YouTube channel, tracking competitor channel content, extracting video metrics such as views likes comments, retrieving subscriber count and channel info, monitoring posting cadence of a YouTube channel, gathering video data for content strategy analysis, getting earliest videos of a YouTube creator, analyzing engagement signals across a full channel, and downloading structured YouTube video details without manual scraping.
skill.summary: Extract structured YouTube channel and video metrics via BrowserAct API without manual scraping
skill.author: browser-act

security_audit.summary: Static scanner flagged 52 potential issues, but all findings are false positives. Network access is legitimate BrowserAct API calls. Environment variable access is standard API key retrieval. External command warnings triggered by bash examples in documentation, not actual execution. No cryptographic operations, no malicious intent, no prompt injection detected.

low_findings.title: Network Access via HTTP Requests
low_findings.description: Script uses Python requests library to call BrowserAct API. This is intentional and required for the skill's core functionality.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: requests.post and requests.get are direct API calls to browseract.com. No exfiltration or malicious network behavior detected.

low_findings.title: Environment Variable Access for API Key
low_findings.description: Script retrieves BROWSERACT_API_KEY from environment. Standard credential management pattern for third-party API authentication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: os.getenv is standard Python pattern for API key retrieval. Key is used only for BrowserAct API authentication, not sent to external servers.

content.user_title: Extract YouTube channel and video metrics automatically
content.value_statement: Manually scraping YouTube data is time-consuming and often blocked by CAPTCHA or geo-restrictions. This skill automates extraction of channel metadata, video details, and engagement metrics via BrowserAct API without manual intervention.
actual_capabilities: Extract channel metadata including subscriber count, channel title, and URL
Fetch video lists with metrics: view count, like count, comment count, publish date
Filter videos by type: Latest, Popular, or Earliest ordering
Retrieve video duration and direct video URLs for further processing
Poll task status with real-time progress updates during execution
Handle API authentication via environment variable with clear error messages
limitations: Requires valid BrowserAct API key (BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable)
Task execution may take several minutes for large channels due to polling
Data quality depends on BrowserAct template availability and YouTube page structure
No support for private or age-restricted content

use_cases.title: Competitor Channel Monitoring
use_cases.description: Track competitor YouTube channels by extracting their video performance metrics. Monitor posting frequency, engagement rates, and subscriber growth to inform your content strategy.

use_cases.title: Creator Research and Analysis
use_cases.description: Analyze engagement patterns across creator channels. Identify which video types (popular vs latest) drive most views, and correlate subscriber counts with video performance.

use_cases.title: Automated Content Reporting
use_cases.description: Automate YouTube channel monitoring for regular reporting. Extract structured data directly into databases or CRM systems without manual CSV exports.

prompt_templates.title: Get popular videos from a channel
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the youtube-video-api-skill to extract the popular videos from the YouTube channel at [CHANNEL_URL] and show me their view counts and engagement metrics.
prompt_templates.scenario: When you need quick overview of trending content on a specific channel

prompt_templates.title: Track latest uploads
prompt_templates.prompt: Apply the youtube-video-api-skill to get the latest 10 videos from [CHANNEL_URL] including their publish dates and view counts.
prompt_templates.scenario: When monitoring a competitor for new content releases

prompt_templates.title: Channel performance comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the youtube-video-api-skill to extract metrics from [CHANNEL_URL_1] and [CHANNEL_URL_2], then compare their subscriber counts, average view counts, and posting frequency.
prompt_templates.scenario: When comparing performance across multiple channels

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive channel audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Run the youtube-video-api-skill for [CHANNEL_URL] using both Popular and Earliest video types. Generate a summary report with channel info, video counts, and engagement trends.
prompt_templates.scenario: When conducting thorough research on a channel's full history

output_examples.input: Extract the latest 5 videos from the BrowserAct YouTube channel
output_examples.output:
Channel: BrowserAct (https://www.youtube.com/@BrowserAct)

Latest Videos:
1. Video Title - 12,500 views, published 2 days ago
2. Video Title - 8,200 views, published 1 week ago
3. Video Title - 15,700 views, published 2 weeks ago
4. Video Title - 6,400 views, published 3 weeks ago
5. Video Title - 9,100 views, published 1 month ago

output_examples.input: Compare the subscriber counts and top videos between two tech channels
output_examples.output:
Channel A: 450K subscribers, average 25K views per video
Channel B: 320K subscribers, average 18K views per video

Channel A produces 2-3 videos per week with higher engagement rates.

output_examples.input: Get the earliest videos from a channel to understand their growth
output_examples.output:
Earliest Videos from [Channel Name]:
- First video (2018): 500 views
- First year content: average 200 views
- Breakout video (2020): 50K views marked growth inflection
- Current performance: consistent 10K-15K views

best_practices: Always verify the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable is set before invoking the skill to avoid authentication errors
Use the Popular video type as default for competitive analysis, as it shows the most relevant content
Check for 'Invalid authorization' in output first during error handling, as this indicates API key issues requiring user intervention
anti_patterns: Do not pass unvalidated channel URLs directly to the script without verifying they are valid YouTube URLs
Do not retry the script more than once for non-authentication errors, as repeated failures indicate underlying issues
Do not assume the skill can extract private or members-only content; it only accesses publicly visible YouTube data

faq.question: What is BrowserAct and why is it needed?
faq.answer: BrowserAct is a browser automation service that handles CAPTCHA, geo-blocking, and anti-scraping measures. The skill uses BrowserAct API to reliably extract YouTube data without manual scraping challenges.

faq.question: How do I get a BrowserAct API key?
faq.answer: Visit https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations to obtain your API key. Set it as the BROWSERACT_API_KEY environment variable or provide it directly when prompted by the agent.

faq.question: Why does the task take several minutes to complete?
faq.answer: The skill uses automated browser operations to navigate YouTube pages, which requires time for page loading and data extraction. Polling occurs every 10 seconds with a maximum wait of 5 minutes.

faq.question: What video types can I extract?
faq.answer: You can extract videos ordered by Popular (default), Latest, or Earliest. Popular shows highest-viewed content, Latest shows most recent uploads, and Earliest shows oldest videos on the channel.

faq.question: Can I extract data from private or unlisted videos?
faq.answer: No, the skill only accesses publicly visible YouTube content. Private, unlisted, or age-restricted videos cannot be extracted through this skill.

faq.question: How do I handle API key errors?
faq.answer: If you see 'Invalid authorization' in the output, check your BrowserAct API key at https://www.browseract.com/reception/integrations. Do not retry; resolve the key issue first by updating your environment variable.
